step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes a multi-account CI/CD and security architecture for SwiftX. I will create a Developer node pushing code to GitHub. Next, I will add a CI/CD account containing an EKS cluster running Tekton pipelines and ECR for image registry. Multiple target environments exist: Playground EKS (ephemeral feature environments), Development EKS, and Production EKS. I will represent these as separate EKS nodes. AWS Control Tower is used to manage all 14+ accounts. Finally, GuardDuty, VPC Flow Logs, and EKS send logs to a centralized Security Lake. Flows will cover Playground promotion, Prod promotion, and centralized security monitoring.

graph.name: SwiftX Secure Multi-Account CI/CD Pipeline with EKS and AWS Security Lake
graph.notes: SwiftX manages billions in customer funds. They use AWS Control Tower to manage 14+ segregated accounts. Code pipelines run inside EKS using Tekton, promoting signed container images via IAM roles across Playground, Dev, and Prod. Security Lake consolidates audit logs.

nodes.name: SwiftX Engineers
nodes.notes: Developer working on local machine, initiating pull requests and code reviews.

nodes.name: GitHub
nodes.notes: Source control and pull request platform triggering CI/CD pipelines.

nodes.name: CI/CD Account - Tekton EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: Kubernetes cluster running Tekton to build images, run tests, and perform security checks.

nodes.name: Elastic Container Registry (ECR)
nodes.notes: Centralized registry in CI/CD account storing secure, signed container images.

nodes.name: Playground EKS Clusters
nodes.notes: Hosts ephemeral feature-branch environments for product manager and stakeholder review.

nodes.name: Development EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: Initial promotion environment to validate built images before production.

nodes.name: Production EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: Highly secure production workspace running live exchange workloads.

nodes.name: AWS Control Tower
nodes.notes: Manages 14+ AWS accounts to ensure consistent security baselines and configuration.

nodes.name: Amazon Security Lake
nodes.notes: Aggregates security logs globally in a centralized security account.

nodes.name: Amazon GuardDuty
nodes.notes: Monitors threat behavior and forwards events to Security Lake.

nodes.name: VPC Flow Logs
nodes.notes: Collects network traffic details, feeding them to Security Lake.

edges.notes: Developer pushes code to feature branch and opens a PR.
PR triggers Tekton pipeline within EKS.
Tekton builds, tests, and pushes the signed image to ECR.
Promoted via IAM roles to ephemeral Playground environments.
Stakeholders and developers review running feature environments.
Two reviewers approve the PR, prompting a merge to main.
Merge triggers final production pipeline on Tekton.
Builds and stores highly vetted image in ECR.
IAM role-based push to Development environment EKS.
Promoted to Production EKS after passing automated development tests.
GuardDuty logs sent to centralized Security Lake.
VPC Flow Logs forwarded to Security Lake.
Application and audit logs from EKS forwarded to Security Lake.
